title: Visualizing the Business Cycle
thesis_bullets: Hey everyone and thanks for dropping back into the Macroverse.
Today we are going to visualize the business cycle.

Or if you want to read the recent report that we published about a week ago now, the Macro Risk Memo, which I think does a pretty good job of outlining my thoughts on where we are in the business cycle.
But in this video, I want to go through a thoughtful exercise on how we can better visualize them.
The reality is that a lot of millennials, like myself, haven't really experienced a full business cycle.
If you were born in the 90s, which I was, if you were born in the 90s, then you haven't really been an adult when the last major business cycle played out.
Now just because we haven't really seen one, yes there was the pandemic, but that's not really the same thing.
Just because we haven't seen it doesn't mean it won't ever happen.
So what I want to do is try to better visualize them.
Now one of the really interesting things is just when you look at the S&P 500, when we're looking at monthly candles going all the way back to the 1870s, what do you notice?

hypotheses.statement: GOLD continuation edge appears when signal alignment persists on monthly.
hypotheses.rationale: Research and indicator mentions converge on trend-continuation logic with confirmation filters.
hypotheses.falsifiable_test: Backtest GOLD on monthly: require >=55% win rate and positive expectancy over 200+ trades; reject if unmet.

candidate_signals.name: alignment_entry
candidate_signals.description: Enter only when primary trend signal and confirmation filter agree on bar close.

strategy_heuristics_used: Every hypothesis should include explicit regime assumptions and invalidate conditions.
Separate signal logic from execution logic to reduce confounded performance attribution.
Require risk gating concepts before scaling complexity in entry or exit rules.
automation_heuristics_used: Keep human approval checkpoints for high-impact automation and deployment transitions.
Enforce deterministic session gating before any live-capable execution pathway is enabled.
Invest first in telemetry for risk, latency, and execution drift before adding new automation features.
